Robert,

While you are there and have somewhat unlimited amounts, could you try levitating a magnet,with a bigger piece of graphite,  kinda like bismuth

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Those are harsh conditions - I am pretty sure it would destroy the unique properties - but you can't say anything with out trying it. I will try it out

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I am glad to have independent confirmation - I like the potential with this material. Thanks for sharing

author: @MrD4N1EL7
text:
Great to hear! But if you use this graphite to make GO do you think it may loose it's diamagnetic properties then? 
I say this because maybe we can sandwich some copper leaf in between stacked layers, surround that with magnets and build ourselves a little generator.
And possibly even a small solar tracker which itself could be powered by graphene solar cells.

author: @Allwestconstruction
text:
Hi Robert,

Just some very brief tests with the graphite I received, and it is what you say it is, I was able to replicate your  Diamagnetic tests with magnets 
This graphite can be easily flaked very thin, and will actually bend like foil, you can also cut it with scissors 

Best of luck

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: it's in th e middle of sri lanka - I know nothing about the geological history of the area but you are right it would be interesting to find out
